questions.question: 中文导读：What does a p-value of 0.03 mean in a hypothesis test?
options: 中文选项：There is a 3% probability the null hypothesis is true
中文选项：There is a 3% probability of seeing data this extreme if the null hypothesis were true
中文选项：The model improved by 3%
中文选项：97% of the data supports the alternative hypothesis
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The p-value is the probability of observing data as extreme as what you got, assuming the null hypothesis is true. It is NOT the probability that H0 is true — this is the single most common misunderstanding in statistics.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why do you divide by (n-1) instead of n when computing sample variance?
options: 中文选项：It makes the computation faster
中文选项：It accounts for the fact that the sample mean is not the true population mean (Bessel's correction)
中文选项：It converts the variance to standard deviation
中文选项：It only applies when the sample size is odd
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Bessel's correction (dividing by n-1) compensates for the bias introduced by using the sample mean instead of the true population mean. Without it, sample variance systematically underestimates the true population variance.

questions.question: 中文导读：You test 20 different model configurations at alpha = 0.05. What is the approximate probability of at least one false positive?
options: 中文选项：5%
中文选项：25%
中文选项：64%
中文选项：95%
questions.explanation: 中文解析：P(at least one false positive) = 1 - (1 - 0.05)^20 = 1 - 0.95^20 ≈ 0.64 (64%). This is the multiple comparison problem. Bonferroni correction addresses it by testing each at alpha/20 = 0.0025.

questions.question: 中文导读：Model A scores 0.9234 and Model B scores 0.9237 on 1 million test samples with p-value = 0.001. What should you conclude?
options: 中文选项：Model B is significantly better and should be deployed immediately
中文选项：The difference is statistically significant but a 0.03% improvement may not be practically significant
中文选项：The test is invalid because the sample size is too large
中文选项：Model A is better because it was tested first
questions.explanation: 中文解析：With 1 million samples, even trivially small differences become statistically significant. The p-value confirms the difference is real, but the effect size (0.03% accuracy gain) may not justify the engineering cost of deployment. Always report both p-value and effect size.

questions.question: 中文导读：What advantage does bootstrap have over the paired t-test for comparing two ML models?
options: 中文选项：Bootstrap always produces smaller p-values
中文选项：Bootstrap requires no distributional assumptions and works for any metric (AUC, F1, median)
中文选项：Bootstrap needs fewer samples to reach significance
中文选项：Bootstrap can only be used with neural networks
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Bootstrap resampling estimates the sampling distribution of any statistic by resampling with replacement. Unlike the t-test, it does not assume normality. It works for any metric — AUC, F1, precision@k, median — without needing a closed-form formula.
